channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AI reshapes public service hiring as automation challenges traditional recruitment methods
item.description: Generative AI has transformed the Australian Public Service (APS) hiring landscape, overwhelming recruitment systems with automated applications. Candidates are increasingly using platforms like ChatG...

item.title: Rico Hizon Advocates for Collective Action Against Disinformation
item.description: Rico Hizon, recipient of the Asia's Golden Icon award, emphasized the urgent need for collective action to combat fake news and misinformation. In a recent statement, he urged media professionals, cit...

item.title: ICE Officer Shoots and Kills Mexican Immigrant During Traffic Stop in Houston
item.description: On July 7, 2026, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican immigrant, during a traffic stop arrest attempt in Houston. Federal authorities con...

item.title: Saskatchewan Female Athletes Benefit from Growing Flag Football Opportunities
item.description: Women’s flag football is gaining momentum in Saskatchewan, offering new pathways for female athletes. Ella Sowden, a Saskatoon native, recently secured one of only two NFL and RCX Sports Foundation In...

item.title: Baba Levo Alleges Bribery in Court Testimony Sparks Legal Scrutiny
item.description: Tanzanian musician Baba Levo has alleged that he was solicited for a bribe of 100,000 Tanzanian shillings during a court case, adding complexity to proceedings that have drawn public attention. The te...

item.title: DOJ Threatens Election Officials with Criminal Charges Over Noncitizen Voting
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) under President Donald Trump sent letters to election officials in Michigan, Nevada, and Utah, warning them of potential criminal prosecution if they fail to prove comp...

item.title: Refugees in Durban Seek Safe Shelter Amid Threats of Violence
item.description: Approximately 400 legally documented refugees have been camping outside Durban's Refugee Reception Centre for a month, fleeing xenophobic violence and unable to return home. The group, predominantly f...

item.title: Isa Ashiru Meets Ex-Governor El-Rufai Amid Controversy Over Leaked Audio
item.description: Governorship candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Kaduna State, Isa Ashiru Kudan, met with former governor Nasir El-Rufai amid controversy surrounding a leaked audio recording alleged...

item.title: US launches strikes on Iran after attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States has resumed military strikes against Iran following a series of attacks by Tehran on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil and gas transporta...

item.title: Lagos Convicts 16 for Illegal Waste Disposal
item.description: The Lagos State Government has convicted 16 individuals for illegal waste disposal and related sanitation offences. The offenders were arraigned before the Oshodi Special Offences and Mobile Court on ...

item.title: FIFA's mandatory cooling breaks in World Cup matches spark debate over athlete safety and game integrity
item.description: FIFA's decision to mandate cooling breaks in all World Cup matches has sparked controversy, with fans criticizing the pauses as disruptive to the game's flow. However, Dr. Bert Mandelbaum, the U.S. me...

item.title: Modi urges UN Security Council reforms, stronger India-Indonesia partnership and enhanced Indo-Pacific cooperation during Indonesia visit
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi called for urgent reforms to the United Nations Security Council while addressing lawmakers at the Indonesian Parliament, stating that developing countries deserve greater...

item.title: PAGASA Issues Wind Signal for Super Typhoon Bavi in Cagayan
item.description: Super Typhoon Bavi, which has been tracking in the Philippine area of responsibility, has been assigned the local name 'Inday' by the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Admi...

item.title: Air Canada Adjusts U.S. Flights Amid Fuel Costs and Declining Demand
item.description: Air Canada has announced plans to cut or delay eight U.S.-bound routes this winter, citing high jet fuel costs and reduced demand as key factors. The airline is particularly impacting flights to the A...

item.title: NYC high-rise evacuation as structural issues prompt collapse zone
item.description: A high-rise building under construction in Midtown East, New York City, was evacuated after reports of structural instability, prompting emergency responders to establish a collapse zone. The 37-story...

item.title: Federal judge blocks DOJ subpoena over expired election investigation
item.description: A federal judge appointed by former President Donald Trump has blocked the Justice Department's attempt to subpoena extensive records from Fulton County election workers related to the 2020 presidenti...

item.title: 30-year-old man sentenced to 30 years for rape and robbery in Freedom Park
item.description: A 30-year-old man, Thabang Letsholo, has been handed an effective 30-year direct imprisonment sentence by the Tlhabane Magistrates' Court for raping a woman and robbing a young couple in Freedom Park ...

item.title: B.C. Nurses' Union expands job action after alleging employer intimidation and filing labour board complaint
item.description: The BC Nurses' Union says it will broaden its job action during the week after members reported what the union describes as intimidation by employers while participating in labour action. As part of t...

item.title: Cast of “Miss Behave” shares honest answers in ABS-CBN Rapid Fire quiz
item.description: ABS-CBN released a feature highlighting the cast of the television project “Miss Behave” as they took part in a Rapid Fire quiz that allowed fans to get to know them beyond their on-screen roles. The ...

item.title: DA challenges Tshwane deputy mayor's code of conduct penalty in court
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has initiated legal action against Tshwane deputy mayor and finance MMC Eugene Modise, seeking to overturn a two-month salary fine imposed for breaching the Councillors’ C...
